lead_headline: Three new restaurant leads surfaced overnight—Wapos Cantina in Rosedale, Mendocino Farms at Union Station, and a West Colfax food truck closure—while foreclosure filings jumped 67% to 82 cases worth $33.3M.

restaurant.blurb: 3 new leads detected overnight, bringing the active pipeline to 19 leads (7 openings, 12 closures). Fresh intel: Wapos Cantina opened in Rosedale's former El Tepehuan spot, Mendocino Farms debuted its third Denver location at Union Station, and Powered By Besitos Food Truck closed on West Colfax. Also flagged: 303 Ramen in Regis suffered an attic fire March 3, and New Dragon in Berkeley appears shuttered after 13 years. Capitol Hill's Colfax and Cream folded after just two months.

foreclosure.blurb: 82 new filings hit the docket this week—up 67% from last week's 49 and 19% above the prior week's 69—totaling $33.3M in exposure. Top filing: another $8.1M case at 7380 S. Clinton in Arapahoe County. Weld County leads active cases with 315, trailed by Arapahoe's 290. 44 auctions scheduled for the next seven days. Filings have now climbed two consecutive weeks after a mid-February dip.

reputation.blurb: 9 mentions yesterday brought the seven-day total to 29 citations across 10 sources—still riding last week's inaugural tracking surge (zero the prior period). The Denver Post remains the top citing outlet with 11 pickups this week, followed by Hoodline (4) and Instagram (4). Notable Wednesday hits: Post coverage of Northfield duplexes and a remodel firm's legal drama. Google News RSS accounted for 28 of 29 discoveries; one came via Reddit.

subscriptions.blurb: Net −4 yesterday (3 new, 7 canceled) erased this week's modest gains, leaving March at net zero through six days—flat at 0.0/day. The 90-day cumulative sits at +75 net. Recent two-week pattern shows volatility: Feb 24's −9 day was offset by gains Feb 23 (+5) and Feb 27 (+3), but this week opened soft with back-to-back negative days (−1, −4). Longer-term YTD pace of +1.1/day remains intact despite the churn.
